title: Task 17 Follow-Up: Theoretical Analysis of State Parameter Adaptation (eps, tau, delta)

content.objective: Provide Principle II-compliant intrinsic signals for eps/tau/delta per-cell adaptation, assess c006/c007 risks, and account for frozen frame impact
content.summary: All three state parameters (eps, tau, delta) are BINDING (d > 0.5 from Task #19). This analysis designs intrinsic signals for each, confirming Principle II compliance and demonstrating that c006/c007 do NOT apply (they block global coupling, not local state mechanisms).

parameter_1_epsilon.logic: High correlation → pulling helps MI, keep/increase eps[i]. Low/negative correlation → pulling harms, decrease eps[i].
parameter_1_epsilon.principle_ii: PASSES - signal arises from internal pull computation and resp_z tracking, no external measurement
parameter_1_epsilon.c006_c007_risk: LOW - eps is additive LOCAL correction (post-computation), not multiplicative GLOBAL coupling in core equation. Per-cell eps ENABLES specialization.
parameter_1_epsilon.frozen_frame: Eliminated: global eps. Introduced: eps[NC], eps_clip_lo, eps_clip_hi, eps_meta, eps_signal_weight. Net: +4
parameter_1_epsilon.priority: TIER 1 (highest) - strongest signal clarity, best balance of binding + simplicity

parameter_2_delta.current_role: State mixing ratio memory vs computation (line 227)
parameter_2_delta.logic: High plasticity → cell adapting fast, increase delta to let computation dominate. Low plasticity → alpha stable, decrease delta for memory.
parameter_2_delta.principle_ii: PASSES - signal derives from alpha dynamics (internal), no external fitness measurement
parameter_2_delta.c006_c007_risk: LOW - delta is LOCAL state mixing, not GLOBAL coupling. No coupling term to destroy. c006 specific to beta/gamma in core equation.
parameter_2_delta.frozen_frame: Eliminated: global delta. Introduced: delta[NC], delta_clip_lo, delta_clip_hi, delta_meta, plasticity_window. Net: +4
parameter_2_delta.priority: TIER 2 (medium) - simpler signal, lower implementation cost, good fallback

parameter_3_tau.logic: High entropy (diffuse attention) → increase tau to sharpen. Low entropy (sharp) → decrease tau to explore.
parameter_3_tau.principle_ii: PASSES - entropy computed from internal attention weights, no external measurement
parameter_3_tau.c006_c007_risk: LOW - attention already per-cell, tau controls individual cell's softmax sharpness, not any shared coupling
parameter_3_tau.frozen_frame: Eliminated: global tau. Introduced: tau[NC], tau_clip_lo, tau_clip_hi, tau_meta, entropy_target. Net: +4
parameter_3_tau.priority: TIER 3 (lower) - strongest effect but most complex implementation, try last

c006_c007_assessment.context: c006/c007 block per-cell decomposition of beta/gamma (global multiplicative coupling in core equation)
c006_c007_assessment.generalization_test: Do c006/c007 apply to eps, tau, delta?
c006_c007_assessment.eps_result: NO - eps is additive LOCAL correction, not multiplicative GLOBAL coupling. Per-cell eps enables specialization.
c006_c007_assessment.delta_result: NO - delta is LOCAL state mixing, no coupling term. c006 specific to core equation coupling.
c006_c007_assessment.tau_result: NO - attention already per-cell computed. Per-cell tau changes individual cell focus, not shared coupling.
c006_c007_assessment.key_insight: c006/c007 are SPECIFIC constraints on global coupling in core equation. They do NOT generalize to local or post-computation parameters. This is a critical distinction that validates per-cell state parameter adaptation.

priority_ranking.tier_1_epsilon: d=-0.663, HIGH signal clarity, MEDIUM complexity, Tier 1 for Phase 2
priority_ranking.tier_2_delta: d=-0.502, MEDIUM clarity, LOW complexity, fallback to Tier 1
priority_ranking.tier_3_tau: d=-0.851 (strongest), MEDIUM clarity, HIGH complexity, try after Tier 1-2

implications: Stage 4 NOT vacuous - binding constraints found in state dynamics layer
Principle II satisfiable - all signals arise from internal computation
Frozen frame CAN shrink via state parameter adaptation
c006/c007 specificity enables safe per-cell decomposition of local mechanisms
